industry_note: Wine/beverage is the PRIMARY expertise anchor for every manager and the non-negotiable regulatory vertical the company is being built against. To prevent managers from narrowing their expertise to only this field, every manager also declares a serendipitous secondary background drawn from an unrelated prior-work domain, recorded in their skill-scope.md. The secondary is a cognitive lens for problem-solving, never a second operational duty.
geography: Operational base: south-central Pennsylvania, rural, with direct access to a state-licensed custom-crush facility and three existing vineyard partnerships in Lancaster and York counties. No physical tasting room yet; DTC-first strategy.
regulatory_context: Federal TTB (Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau)
Pennsylvania Liquor Control Board (PLCB) and Pennsylvania three-tier system
COLA (Certificate of Label Approval) requirements for every SKU
State-by-state DTC shipping permits (PA outbound, plus priority targets NY, NJ, VA, MD)
PLCB's Licensee Service Center processes for Limited Winery License
priorities: Secure Pennsylvania Limited Winery License and custom-crush agreement in time for the 2026 harvest window
Produce a first commercial vintage under the Quarry Ridge label (target: 400 cases across two SKUs)
Establish DTC sales infrastructure: Shopify storefront, ShipCompliant for compliance, fulfillment partner in-state
Define the product lineup (varietals, price point, format) consistent with natural-process positioning
settled_convictions: Brand voice is grounded, pragmatic, place-based. Not luxury, not folksy, not ironic.
Natural-process positioning: native yeasts, minimal intervention, low sulfur. This is a commitment, not a marketing angle.
No outside equity investors. Debt, grants, and revenue-funded growth only.
Operational base is Pennsylvania. Do not propose relocating operations to a larger wine-producing state.
Direct-to-consumer first. Wholesale distribution is a later-year consideration, not a launch channel.
hard_constraints: Never market to or involve anyone under 21
Never violate TTB labeling, COLA, or federal alcohol regulations
Never violate Pennsylvania state licensing requirements
Never commit company funds without the founder's explicit approval
Never publish copy or imagery that contradicts the natural-process positioning
